summary_one_line: Input must carry the literal 'sRGB' chunk-type FOURCC (so png_handle_sRGB is reached) with a chunk-length field not equal to 1.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte chunk-type tag at offset 0x35-0x38 must equal the ASCII FOURCC 'sRGB' (73 52 47 42) so the PNG chunk dispatcher routes into png_handle_sRGB, after which the 4-byte big-endian chunk-length field at 0x31-0x34 is anything other than 1, taking the `length != 1` true side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog/vpc winning seeds carry the exact 'sRGB' tag (offset 0x35-0x38 = 73 52 47 42) and reach png_handle_sRGB (W hits 78 vs L hits 24); I2SRandReplace splices the logged 'sRGB' string operand from the chunk-type comparison into the input so the dispatcher matches, then mutates the length away from 1 (W bytes at 0x34 include 28/86/ef besides 01).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive/value_profile have no I2S substitution and must stumble onto both the FOURCC match and a non-1 length by chance; their blocking seeds keep the length low byte fixed at 01 at offset 0x34 (L = 01 x21) so length stays 1 and the true side is never taken (loser resolved 1/10, blocked 9/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace: the chunk-type strcmp/memcmp against "sRGB" is logged into the CmpLogObserver buffer and the mutator substitutes that operand into the input at the matching offset, opening the path to png_handle_sRGB where the length check can then diverge from 1. value_profile alone lacks I2S, so vpc>vp isolates the same I2S substitution.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side target — an 'sRGB'-typed chunk reaching png_handle_sRGB with chunk length != 1 — but reached incrementally as the input progresses through the chunk-dispatch call path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching png_handle_sRGB through a new call context registers as new coverage and the intermediate seed is retained, letting the campaign build toward the length!=1 true side (winner resolved 10/10, branch hit 11 times in W).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: plain naive records only bare edges, so a deeper chunk-dispatch path that re-hits already-covered edges yields no new coverage and the progressing seed is discarded; naive never retains the stepping stones and stays blocked (resolved 1/10, blocked 9/10, avg 21.80h blocked).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook makes (call-context, edge) pairs the coverage unit, so the same chunk-dispatch edge counts as new when reached via a fresh caller chain into png_handle_sRGB; this finer feedback preserves intermediate inputs that drive toward the length!=1 branch, which bare-edge naive cannot.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of `if (length != 1)` in png_handle_sRGB and is globally blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:    if (length != 1)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The 'sRGB' FOURCC chunk-type tag appears at offset 0x35-0x38 in the seeds.
Winner seeds vary the chunk-length low byte at offset 0x34 while loser seeds keep it fixed at 01.
The winner reaches png_handle_sRGB far more than the loser (78 vs 24 hits).
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, isolating the I2S delta for the vpc>vp pair.
naive_ctx folds call context into the edge-map index so (call-context, edge) pairs are new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: plain naive records only bare-edge hit-count buckets with no context indexing.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins 10/10 while naive loses 9/10 blocked, with naive averaging 21.80h blocked.
Loser naive/value_profile seeds keep the length field at 1 and never take the true side (1/10 resolved).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3980 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed <ID>` on cmplog winners 0e2bfd4799a49607 (I2S-floor dash rows at depths 8 and 9), ba841eb6628dbbbf (depth 9), 56539fec6a1dd836 (depth 10), and vpc winner 359682747c363d6f (depth 8). All cmplog/vpc winning chains contain ParentInfo-only `mutation_op = -` ancestors, the I2S-floor lower-bound signal for an I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the sRGB chunk-type FOURCC is reached without any byte-literal comparison (e.g. dispatch by a runtime-computed index) and cmplog still outpaces naive at taking the length!=1 branch, showing the divergence is not driven by I2S splicing the logged 'sRGB' operand.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff shows the length low byte at 0x34 varying in W vs fixed 01 in L, but it does not directly prove I2S spliced the 'sRGB' FOURCC rather than the FOURCC already being present in the corpus seed prefix.
